title: Is Patriots’ 2026 schedule actually easier than many expected? This win-projection metric says so.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots have the 12th-easiest 2026 schedule, according to Sharp Football Analysis.
Schedule includes teams like the Bills, Chiefs, and Packers but also struggling teams like Jets and Raiders.
The Patriots face eight 2025 playoff teams, including division champs like Steelers and Jaguars.

title: Could Patriots reunite with Stefon Diggs? One Patriots writer doesn’t expect it.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Stefon Diggs remains unsigned after surpassing 1,000 receiving yards with the Patriots in 2025.
Patriots reporter Phil Perry doubts a Diggs return due to the team's interest in A.J. Brown.
The Patriots released Diggs for salary cap reasons, despite his chemistry with QB Drake Maye.

title: Man, 31, killed in New Bedford shooting
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Steven Coan, 31, was found with multiple gunshot wounds inside his car in New Bedford on May 6.
Coan was taken to St. Luke's Hospital where he was pronounced dead, authorities reported.
The shooting is being treated as a homicide, with State and New Bedford police investigating.

title: Hayden, Rollins, and third Suffolk DA candidate Champion secure enough signatures to appear on ballot
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kevin Hayden, Rachael Rollins, and Linda Champion secured over 1,000 signatures to qualify for the Suffolk DA primary ballot.
Rollins collected the most verified signatures in Boston with 1,430, followed by Champion with 1,349, and Hayden with 1,249.
The primary race will occur on September 1, with Hayden facing controversy over past decisions and Rollins dealing with earlier ethics violations.

title: These are Boston’s highest-paid tech CEOs, BBJ data shows
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Wayfair CEO Niraj Shah's total compensation jumped 990% to $280.9 million in 2025, topping Boston's tech CEO pay list.
Analog Devices CEO Vincent Roche earned $27.5 million, ranking second among Boston's highest-paid tech CEOs in 2025.
BBJ ranked Boston tech CEOs by analyzing salaries, stock awards, and compensation from SEC proxy statements.

title: Karen Read’s SUV heads to auction in Woburn. Here’s what readers think it’s worth.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Karen Read's 2021 Lexus LX 570 involved in a murder trial is set for auction in Woburn on May 14.
Readers estimate the SUV could sell from less than $70,000 to over $500,000, depending on its history and appeal.
A jury acquitted Read of murder charges, though she's involved in a wrongful death lawsuit related to John O'Keefe.

title: ‘What happened today cannot stand’: Memorial Drive shooter fired more than 50 rounds before trooper, civilian intervened, DA says
author: Abby Patkin and Molly Farrar
bullets: Shooter on Memorial Drive, Tyler Brown, fired over 50 rounds, injuring two before being subdued.
A State Police trooper and Marine veteran halted the shooter, who was wounded and apprehended.
The injured victims were taken to Boston hospitals with life-threatening injuries; no ongoing public threat reported.

title: Police investigating viral video of apparent food contamination at McDonald’s in Mass.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Southbridge police investigate a viral video showing a McDonald's employee allegedly tampering with food.
The incident led to an internal probe; those involved no longer work at the Southbridge McDonald's.
Local health authorities found no violations; McDonald's prioritizes community safety and coordinates with police.

title: MassINC poll: Many Bay Staters say their quality of life is slipping in Mass.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: MassINC poll shows deep economic pessimism among Massachusetts residents with many facing affordability challenges.
Over half of respondents find housing unaffordable, rising to 65% among those aged 35-44.
Only 27% expect financial improvement, and many feel Massachusetts favors the wealthy over middle-class families.

title: Celtics reportedly tried to land Giannis Antetokounmpo at the trade deadline
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Celtics were among teams that reportedly pursued Giannis Antetokounmpo at the trade deadline, according to ESPN's Shams Charania.
Boston is considering roster changes after recent playoff struggles, emphasized by President Brad Stevens seeking rim-impact players.
Any trade for Antetokounmpo could require significant salary moves and draft assets, with a strong market expected for the Bucks star.
